response.justification: Bogdan is a strong technical generalist with genuine Staff-level experience and directly relevant AI-native engineering work — his current role at re:cinq explicitly involves MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure, which aligns well with the role's core context. His time at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems experience at scale (millions of HTTP requests per second). However, the must-have Python requirement is entirely absent from his resume and skills list, which is a material gap for a role where Python is listed as a core language. His seniority and AI tooling exposure make him worth a screening call to assess the Python gap and stakeholder management depth before ruling him out.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq involves hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, directly matching the AI-native engineering environment
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby with low-latency, asynchronous I/O handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Consistent Staff-level track record across multiple employers with evidence of technical leadership, mentoring, and cross-domain ownership
concerns: Python is listed as a must-have skill but is completely absent from the resume, skills section, and all employment history
No explicit evidence of stakeholder management across engineering, product, and leadership functions at the scale expected for a Staff IC role
Primary language depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and niche DLang; the breadth of languages listed may indicate width over depth in the must-have stack
